items.front: When must radar separation be applied to RNAV aircraft?
items.back: Apply radar separation to all RNAV aircraft at and below FL450 on Q routes or random RNAV routes, excluding oceanic airspace.

items.front: How is target separation measured on radar displays?
items.back: Separate primary targets center-to-center, beacon slashes end-to-end, and on all-digital displays keep digital targets center-to-center without letting them touch.

items.front: What does target resolution require besides keeping targets apart?
items.back: When target resolution is used, traffic advisories and safety alerts are mandatory.

items.front: What is the basic terminal radar-separation minimum in single-sensor ASR or DTAS inside 40 miles?
items.back: Use 3 miles inside 40 miles from the antenna. Beyond that, use 5 miles unless a listed special case applies.

items.front: How may aircraft that are not laterally separated be vertically separated by radar?
items.back: Assign altitudes using valid Mode C and keep the required vertical minima at all times, or reuse an altitude only after the first aircraft has left it under the paragraph's standards.

items.front: When may Mode C not be used to provide vertical separation?
items.back: Do not use Mode C for vertical separation on a cruise clearance, a contact approach, or the other cases specifically barred by the paragraph.

items.front: When can passing or diverging separation replace other approved separation?
items.back: Use it only after the required crossing or passing geometry exists, the courses diverge by the required angle, and the targets are monitored to keep the required spacing increasing.

items.front: What extra radar separation is required for standard formation flights?
items.back: Add 1 mile to the applicable radar minimum for one standard formation, and add 2 miles when separating two standard formations from each other.

items.front: What obstruction-separation minimum applies in terminal radar inside 40 miles?
items.back: Separate aircraft from prominent obstructions by 3 miles inside 40 miles from the antenna, unless a listed fusion or multisensor rule applies.

items.front: What is the main adjacent-airspace rule when radar coordination has not been completed?
items.back: Keep the aircraft separated from the adjacent-airspace boundary by the applicable minima, with larger minima when the adjoining airspace uses nonradar separation.

items.front: What edge-of-scope separation applies inside 40 miles from the antenna?
items.back: Use 3 miles from the edge of scope until nonradar separation is established.

items.front: What correction is required for specified beacon target displacement?
items.back: Add 1 mile to the applicable minimum when separating a displaced beacon target from a primary target, adjacent airspace, obstructions, or terrain.
